name: WebDialog
summary: Deprecated. Please use HtmlDialog that was introduced in SketchUp 2017. The Ruby WebDialog class allows you to create and interact with DHTML dialog boxes from Ruby. If your goal is to simply display a website to your users, consider using openURL, which will show them a web page in their default browser rather than inside a dialog in SketchUp. See this blog post for a detailed, step-by-step example: sketchupapi.blogspot.com/2008/02/sharing-data-between-sketchup-ruby-and.html Under Windows the IE render mode is different in webdialogs than from what you see in the normal browser. It will by default pick an older render mode and different versions of SketchUp will use different modes. In order to reliably control the render mode of your webdialogs under Windows you need to insert a special META compatibility tag: Starting with SketchUp 2013, you can embed a special HTML link in your dialog that will launch Extension Warehouse and show a specified extension's page. This can be useful if your extension has a dependency on another one and you would like to direct the user to install that extension. For example, to launch an extension's page whose URL is: extensions.sketchup.com/en/content/advanced-camera-tools The link would be:

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.signature: WebDialog(dialog_title = "", scrollable = true, pref_key = nil, width = 250, height = 250, left = 0, top = 0, resizable = true)
constructors.summary: Note: Since SU2017 the position and size of the dialog is DPI aware - it will scale to the DPI of the monitor automatically. Specify units as they would be on a traditional low-DPI monitor. Note: The browser which is embedded inside the dialog depends on the user's OS. On Mac, Safari is embedded, while on the PC whatever version of Internet Explorer is installed will be embedded. The new method is used to create a new webdialog.
constructors.remarks: Note: Since SU2017 the position and size of the dialog is DPI aware - it will scale to the DPI of the monitor automatically. Specify units as they would be on a traditional low-DPI monitor. Note: The browser which is embedded inside the dialog depends on the user's OS. On Mac, Safari is embedded, while on the PC whatever version of Internet Explorer is installed will be embedded. The new method is used to create a new webdialog.

params.name: dialog_title
params.doc: The title to be displayed in the webdialog.

params.name: scrollable
params.doc: true if you want to allow scrollbars, false if you do not want to allow scrollbars.

params.name: pref_key
params.doc: The registry entry where the location and size of the dialog will be saved. If preferences_key is not included, the location and size will not be stored.

params.name: width
params.doc: The width of the webdialog.

params.name: height
params.doc: The height of the webdialog.

params.name: left
params.doc: The number of pixels from the left.

params.name: top
params.doc: The number of pixels from the top.

params.name: resizable
params.doc: true if you want the webdialog to be resizable, false if not.

methods.name: add_action_callback
methods.summary: The add_action_callback method establishes a Ruby callback method that your web dialog can call to perform some function.
methods.remarks: The add_action_callback method establishes a Ruby callback method that your web dialog can call to perform some function. Use the skp:callback_method_name to invoke the callback method from your webdialog. Your JavaScript in the webdialog will invoke the callback method with a string representing arguments to the callback method. Note that you're sending data down to Ruby as a single string that's passed via the window.location bar. In Internet Explorer on PC, there is a length limit of 2038 characters for this bar, so if you're needing to pass large data down you might consider using get_element_value to pull in a longer string from a hidden input field in the HTML.

params.name: callback_name
params.doc: The name of the callback method to be invoked from the webdialog.

methods.name: allow_actions_from_host
methods.summary: By default, actions are only allowed on the host where the webdialog is displayed.
methods.remarks: By default, actions are only allowed on the host where the webdialog is displayed. The allow_actions_from_host method is used to selectively allow actions to take place on a host remote from the host where the webdialog exists. If the webdialog is local, no remote host is allowed unless you use this method.

params.name: hostname
params.doc: The name (domain) of the host that your webdialog can access safely.

methods.name: bring_to_front
methods.summary: The bring_to_front method is used to bring the webdialog to the front of all the windows on the desktop.
methods.remarks: The bring_to_front method is used to bring the webdialog to the front of all the windows on the desktop. See show_modal for how to ensure that your WedDialogs are on top.

methods.name: close
methods.summary: The close method is used to close the webdialog.
methods.remarks: The close method is used to close the webdialog.

methods.name: execute_script
methods.summary: The execute_script method is used to execute a JavaScript string on the web dialog.
methods.remarks: The execute_script method is used to execute a JavaScript string on the web dialog.

params.name: script
params.doc: The JavaScript script to execute on the webdialog.

methods.name: get_default_dialog_color
methods.summary: The get_default_dialog_color method is used to get the default dialog color for the web dialog.
methods.remarks: The get_default_dialog_color method is used to get the default dialog color for the web dialog.

returns.doc: a six digit hexidecimal number representing the color

methods.name: get_element_value
methods.summary: The get_element_value method is used to get a value, with a given element_id, from the web dialog's DOM.
methods.remarks: The get_element_value method is used to get a value, with a given element_id, from the web dialog's DOM.

params.name: element_id
params.doc: The name of the element in your HTML code.

returns.doc: a string containing the retrieved value.

methods.name: navigation_buttons_enabled?
methods.summary: The navigation_buttons_enabled? method is used to get whether the home, next, and back buttons are visible at the top of the WebDialog on the mac.
methods.remarks: The navigation_buttons_enabled? method is used to get whether the home, next, and back buttons are visible at the top of the WebDialog on the mac. This method has no use on the PC, as these buttons are never displayed. On the mac, this defaults to true for new WebDialogs.

methods.name: post_url
methods.summary: The post_url method is used to send the data to a url using the HTTP POST method.
methods.remarks: The post_url method is used to send the data to a url using the HTTP POST method.

params.name: url
params.doc: The url to send the data.

params.name: data
params.doc: The data to be sent.

methods.name: screen_scale_factor
methods.summary: The screen_scale_factor method returns the ratio of screen pixels to logical window units (called 'points' on Mac) for the screen this WebDialog is currently in.
methods.remarks: The screen_scale_factor method returns the ratio of screen pixels to logical window units (called 'points' on Mac) for the screen this WebDialog is currently in. On a retina screen Mac, this ratio will be greater than 1.0. On Windows this always return 1.0.

methods.name: set_background_color
methods.summary: The set_background_color method is used to set the background color for the webdialog.
methods.remarks: The set_background_color method is used to set the background color for the webdialog.

params.name: color
params.doc: A six digit hexidecimal color.

methods.name: set_file
methods.summary: The #set_file method is used to identify a local HTML file to display in the webdialog.
methods.remarks: The #set_file method is used to identify a local HTML file to display in the webdialog.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The filename for the webdialog file (HTML file).

params.name: path
params.doc: A path that filename is relative to.

methods.name: set_full_security
methods.summary: The set_full_security method is used to place the WebDialog into a higher security mode where remote URLs and plugins (such as Flash) are not allowed inside the browser.
methods.remarks: The set_full_security method is used to place the WebDialog into a higher security mode where remote URLs and plugins (such as Flash) are not allowed inside the browser. This defaults to false when a new WebDialog is created.

methods.name: set_html
methods.summary: The set_html method is used to load a webdialog with a string of provided HTML.
methods.remarks: The set_html method is used to load a webdialog with a string of provided HTML.

params.name: html_string
params.doc: A string of valid html to display in your webdialog.

methods.name: set_on_close
methods.summary: Note: #close method should not be called from the #set_on_close callback.
methods.remarks: Note: #close method should not be called from the #set_on_close callback. That would make it trigger itself recursively. The set_on_close method is used to establish one or more activities to perform when the dialog closes (such as saving values stored in the dialog).

methods.name: set_position
methods.summary: Note: As of SU2017 this will automatically scale the x and y by the same factor as UI.
methods.remarks: Note: As of SU2017 this will automatically scale the x and y by the same factor as UI.scale_factor. The set_position method is used to set the position of the webdialog relative to the screen, in pixels.

params.name: left
params.doc: The number of pixels from the left.

params.name: top
params.doc: The number of pixels from the top of the screen.

methods.name: set_size
methods.summary: Note: As of SU2017 this will automatically scale the width and height by the same factor as UI.
methods.remarks: Note: As of SU2017 this will automatically scale the width and height by the same factor as UI.scale_factor. The set_size method is used to set the size of the webdialog, in pixels.

params.name: w
params.doc: Width of the webdialog.

params.name: h
params.doc: Height of the webdialog.

methods.name: set_url
methods.summary: The set_url method is used to load a webdialog with the content at a specific URL.
methods.remarks: The set_url method is used to load a webdialog with the content at a specific URL. This method allows you to load web sites in a webdialog.

params.name: url
params.doc: The URL for a specific web site.

methods.name: show
methods.summary: The show method is used to display a non-modal dialog box.
methods.remarks: The show method is used to display a non-modal dialog box.

methods.name: show_modal
methods.summary: The show_modal method is used to display a modal dialog box.
methods.remarks: The show_modal method is used to display a modal dialog box. In SketchUp 6 and 7, this behaves differently on Mac vs. PC. On the PC, it shows a truly modal dialog, meaning so long as the WebDialog is visible, no input can be performed elsewhere inside SketchUp. On the Mac, “modal” WebDialogs do not behave this way, but instead are “always on top” of other windows.

methods.name: visible?
methods.summary: The visible? method is used to tell if the webdialog is currently shown.
methods.remarks: The visible? method is used to tell if the webdialog is currently shown.

methods.name: write_image
methods.signature: write_image(image_path, option, top_left_x, top_left_y, bottom_right_x, bottom_right_y) => Object
methods.summary: The write_image method is used to grab a portion of the web dialog screen and save the image to the given file path.
methods.remarks: The write_image method is used to grab a portion of the web dialog screen and save the image to the given file path.

params.name: image_path
params.doc: The destination path of the saved image.

params.name: option
params.doc: Specifies what method to use when saving the image. For JPG/JPEG images, this specifies the image quality and can range from 1 to 100. For PNG images this specifies the compression algorithm: <4 (best speed), 4-8 (default), or >=9 (best compression).

params.name: top_left_x
params.doc: The x coordinate of the upper left corner of the region to grab.

params.name: top_left_y
params.doc: The x coordinate of the upper left corner of the region to grab.

params.name: bottom_right_x
params.doc: The x coordinate of the lower right corner of the region to grab.

params.name: bottom_right_y
params.doc: The x coordinate of the lower right corner of the region to grab.

properties.name: max_height
properties.summary: The max_height method is used to get the maximum height that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.
properties.remarks: The max_height method is used to get the maximum height that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.

properties.name: max_width
properties.summary: The max_width method is used to get the maximum width that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.
properties.remarks: The max_width method is used to get the maximum width that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.

properties.name: min_height
properties.summary: The min_width method is used to get the minimum height that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.
properties.remarks: The min_width method is used to get the minimum height that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.

properties.name: min_width
properties.summary: The min_width method is used to get the minimum width that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.
properties.remarks: The min_width method is used to get the minimum width that the user is allowed to resize the dialog to.

properties.name: navigation_buttons_enabled
properties.summary: The navigation_buttons_enabled= method is used to set whether the home, next, and back buttons are visible at the top of the WebDialog on the mac.
properties.remarks: The navigation_buttons_enabled= method is used to set whether the home, next, and back buttons are visible at the top of the WebDialog on the mac. This method has no use on the PC, as these buttons are never displayed.
